title: HealthCheck tests inconsistently parsed by the compatibility API
body:
### Issue Description

When creating a container via the Docker API (like Podman Desktop does), if the `Healthcheck.Test` array contains an argument that itself has spaces (like `["CMD", "/usr/bin/mysql", "--protocol=TCP", "--execute=SELECT 1;"]`), Podman splits this argument into multiple arguments (`[..., "--execute=SELECT", "1;"]`). This is inconsistent with Docker's behavior.

I think the issue is caused by how "`CMD`" `HealthCheck.Test` arrays are parsed into a string and then turned back into an array before execution.

In the container creation handler, the `Healthcheck.Test` array from the JSON payload is concatenated into a single, space-separated string. 

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/683e9b2c94a3e1ddc809b060a2373142f7424773/pkg/api/handlers/compat/containers_create.go#L611-L632

This single string is then passed down to `MakeHealthCheckFromCli` in `pkg/specgenutil/specgen.go`. Inside this function, the string is split back into an array using `strings.Fields`, which splits on any whitespace.

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/683e9b2c94a3e1ddc809b060a2373142f7424773/pkg/specgenutil/specgen.go#L960-L975

This will cause any arguments containing spaces to be treated as separate arguments. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Create a minimal container with a healthcheck containing a space

```shell
curl -X POST "http://localhost/containers/create?name=healthcheck-test" \
-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
--unix-socket ~/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman.sock \
-d '{
  "Image": "mysql:latest",
  "Healthcheck": {
    "Test": ["CMD", "/usr/bin/mysql", "--protocol=TCP", "--execute=SELECT 1;"]
  }
}'
```

### Describe the results you received

Inspect the container's spec

```shell
jq '.Config.Healthcheck' \
  <(curl -s "http://localhost/containers/healthcheck-test/json" --unix-socket ~/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman.sock)
```

```json
{
  "Test": [
    "CMD",
    "/usr/bin/mysql",
    "--protocol=TCP",
    "--execute=SELECT",
    "1;"
  ],
  "Interval": 1000000000,
  "Timeout": 5000000000,
  "Retries": 5
}
```

### Describe the results you expected

Here is the output of inspecting the same container created using Docker

```shell
jq '.Config.Healthcheck' \
  <(curl -s "http://localhost/containers/healthcheck-test/json" --unix-socket /var/run/docker.sock)
```

```json
{
  "Test": [
    "CMD",
    "/usr/bin/mysql",
    "--protocol=TCP",
    "--execute=SELECT 1;"
  ],
  "Interval": 1000000000,
  "Timeout": 5000000000,
  "Retries": 5
}
```

comments.body: i would tend to agree that this is a bug, nice report btw,  thanks @Thomascountz
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
So glad this wasn't just me! Took me what felt like an hour to track down why this was being weird for me! Thanks for the bug report!

As an aside, I haven't checked the difference in the code path, but when I add the same health check via `HEALTHCHECK` in a Dockerfile (as in, using the Docker extension to the OCI standard that enables specifying HEALTHCHECK), Podman parses that just fine and doesn't split on spaces.

> As an aside, I haven't checked the difference in the code path, but when I add the same health check via HEALTHCHECK in a Dockerfile (as in, using the Docker extension to the OCI standard that enables specifying HEALTHCHECK), Podman parses that just fine and doesn't split on spaces.

I think it's because Dockfiles are handled through https://github.com/containers/buildah whereas the issue here is with the CLI/API.

Buildah's `HealthConfig` type preserves the entire `"Test"` value as a `[]string` slice:

https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/fb473e4d538f693f8b3ee3f8f2ed93a2abed5064/docker/types.go#L52-L71

When building the spec, the whole slice is cloned:

https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/fb473e4d538f693f8b3ee3f8f2ed93a2abed5064/config.go#L694-L715
